 {"id":"wa-upg.5.6","title":"E2E: large transcript search remains fast (verbose perf artifacts)","description":"# Task: E2E large transcript search performance

## Goal
Provide an end-to-end scenario that exercises storage + indexing + search under realistic scale.

## Requirements
- Generate or replay a large transcript (prefer fixtures/synthetic to avoid nondeterminism).
- Run representative queries:
  - short term
  - multi-word
  - scoped by pane/time
- Capture perf artifacts:
  - elapsed times
  - index lag signals
  - DB size snapshot

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E produces stable metrics and flags regressions clearly.

 {"id":"wa-upg.5.7","title":"Docs: storage/index tuning + maintenance commands","description":"# Task: Document storage/indexing tuning

## Goal
Make storage behavior predictable and tunable for power users.

## Requirements
- Document:
  - how WAL/checkpointing works in wa
  - how to interpret index lag signals
  - recommended maintenance commands (explicit vacuum/optimize)
  - how to collect perf evidence for bug reports

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can self-diagnose slow search and provide actionable data in an issue.

 {"id":"wa-upg.6","title":"[EPIC] Risk-scored policy decisions (safer defaults + better explanations)","description":"# [EPIC] Risk-scored policy decisions

## Mission
Upgrade PolicyEngine from a flat allow/deny/require-approval to a system that also computes a **risk score** and explains the factors.

This improves:
- safety defaults
- approval UX (users approve "this plan" with a clear risk summary)
- agent autonomy (low-risk actions can proceed; high-risk actions are gated)

## Why This Matters
Binary policies force awkward tradeoffs:
- too strict → tool feels unusable
- too loose → tool becomes dangerous

A risk score allows "safe-by-default" behavior that is still ergonomic:
- low risk: allow
- medium risk: require approval
- high risk: deny or require stronger confirmation

## Scope
- Add risk metadata to policy decisions:
  - `risk_score` (0..100)
  - `risk_factors[]` (stable IDs)
  - `risk_summary` (human-readable)
- Make risk deterministic and explainable.
- Allow config to tune thresholds and weights.

## Success Criteria
- Users can see *why* an action is risky.
- Approval flows surface risk clearly.
- Risk model does not rely on nondeterministic signals.

## Testing
- Unit tests for risk scoring matrix and determinism.
- Integration tests for policy decisions emitted into audit + explainability surfaces.
- E2E: risky send requires approval, low-risk send does not.

## Acceptance Criteria
- PolicyEngine emits stable risk metadata.
- `wa why` (and robot surfaces) can present risk factors.
- Tests and E2E validate correctness and stability.

-{"id":"wa-upg.6.1","title":"Design: risk model (factors, weights, thresholds, config)","description":"# Task: Design policy risk model

## Goal
Define the deterministic risk scoring model used by PolicyEngine.

## Requirements
- Define risk factors (stable IDs) such as:
  - action kind (SendText vs Query)
  - pane state (AltScreen, CommandRunning, PromptActive)
  - recent GAP / uncertain state
  - command content heuristics (destructive-looking tokens)
  - target scope (single pane vs broadcast)
- Define scoring:
  - weights per factor
  - thresholds mapping to decision suggestions
- Config overrides:
  - allow tuning thresholds/weights
  - allow policy rules to pin decisions regardless of score
- Explainability:
  - each factor must map to a human-readable explanation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Risk model is deterministic, explainable, and implementable.
","notes":"2026-01-28: Created comprehensive design doc at docs/risk-model-design.md covering:
- Risk factors registry with stable IDs (state, action, context, content categories)
- Scoring algorithm (additive, capped at 100)
- Decision thresholds (low/medium/elevated/high)
- Config schema for weights and overrides
- Explainability integration (factor explanations, wa why)
- Data structures (RiskConfig, RiskFactor, RiskScore, AppliedFactor)
- Integration points (PolicyEngine, wa why)
- Testing requirements
Ready for implementation review.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyCat","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:42:32.740032396Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:53:39.588557288Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-design","area-safety","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.6.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-upg.6.1","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:11Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"wa-upg.6.1","title":"Design: risk model (factors, weights, thresholds, config)","description":"# Task: Design policy risk model

## Goal
Define the deterministic risk scoring model used by PolicyEngine.

## Requirements
- Define risk factors (stable IDs) such as:
  - action kind (SendText vs Query)
  - pane state (AltScreen, CommandRunning, PromptActive)
  - recent GAP / uncertain state
  - command content heuristics (destructive-looking tokens)
  - target scope (single pane vs broadcast)
- Define scoring:
  - weights per factor
  - thresholds mapping to decision suggestions
- Config overrides:
  - allow tuning thresholds/weights
  - allow policy rules to pin decisions regardless of score
- Explainability:
  - each factor must map to a human-readable explanation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Risk model is deterministic, explainable, and implementable.

 {"id":"wa-upg.6.2","title":"Implement risk scoring in PolicyEngine (risk_score + factors)","description":"# Task: Implement PolicyEngine risk scoring

## Goal
Add risk scoring output to PolicyEngine decisions.

## Requirements
- Extend decision type to include:
  - risk_score (0..100)
  - risk_factors (stable IDs + metadata)
  - risk_summary (short string)
- Ensure determinism:
  - stable ordering of factors
  - no wall-clock inputs
- Ensure safety:
  - risk scoring must not leak secrets (redaction still applies)

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - matrix tests for common combinations
  - determinism tests (same inputs → same outputs)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Risk metadata is present and stable for all policy decisions.

 {"id":"wa-upg.6.3","title":"Integrate risk into wa why/errors/robot outputs (progressive disclosure)","description":"# Task: Surface risk in explainability outputs

## Goal
Make risk scoring useful to users by surfacing it clearly in:
- `wa why`
- enriched errors
- robot outputs

## Requirements
- Output conventions:
  - show a short risk summary by default
  - allow deep dive (list factors with explanations)
- Machine outputs:
  - include risk_score + factors in JSON (stable schema)
- UX:
  - include suggested next steps (e.g., "requires approval" with reason)

## Testing
- Output contract tests for JSON schema.
- Snapshot/golden tests for TTY/plain output stability.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users see a clear reason *and* a clear risk summary.
- Robots can programmatically branch based on risk score and factor IDs.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:43:01.219596324Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:40:39.987222120Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-explainability","area-robot","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-upg.6.3","depends_on_id":"wa-upg.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:11Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-upg.6.4","title":"Tests: risk scoring matrix + output stability","description":"# Task: Tests for policy risk scoring

## Goal
Lock in the risk model and prevent regressions.

## Requirements
- Unit tests:
  - risk scoring matrix for representative conditions
  - stable factor ordering
- Integration/output tests:
  - JSON schema validation includes risk fields
  - `wa why` output includes risk summary and factors in verbose mode

## Acceptance Criteria
- Risk score and factors are stable across runs and changes are intentional.
